name: Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas
name_original: Arrabalde — circular por la Sierra de las Labradas
region_intro: Hiking around Arrabalde offers diverse trails through varied landscapes. The region features river valleys, such as those of the Eria and Órbigo, alongside elevated areas like the Sierra de las Labradas. Hikers can expect a mix of gentle paths and routes with moderate elevation changes, providing opportunities to explore the local terrain. Best hiking trails around Arrabalde The most popular hiking route is Basilia's Balcony and Coto, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes viewpoints and natural features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This trail follows the course of two rivers, offering waterside scenery. Local hikers also love the Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra de las Labradas, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Arrabalde is defined by riverbanks, elevated viewpoints, and the Sierra de las Labradas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate hikes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 12 hikers have used komoot to explore Arrabalde's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Arrabalde?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Arrabalde, offering a variety of experiences through river valleys and elevated areas like the Sierra de las Labradas.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Arrabalde?
A: Yes, Arrabalde offers 2 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed stroll. These trails generally feature gentle paths with minimal elevation changes.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Arrabalde?
A: Hike durations vary, but many popular routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305941795/basilia-s-balcony-and-coto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilia's Balcony and Coto&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 2 to 3 hours. Longer moderate trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022487025/banks-of-the-eria-and-orbigo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 3 to 4 hours.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Arrabalde?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the region's easier trails and those along riverbanks, like parts of the Eria and Órbigo, are generally suitable for families. Look for routes with shorter distances and less elevation gain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Arrabalde?
A: Most trails in Arrabalde are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near roads. Be sure to carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arrabalde?
A: Spring and autumn are ideal for hiking in Arrabalde, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn showcases vibrant foliage. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are preferable.

Q: Which trails offer the best viewpoints in Arrabalde?
A: For scenic vistas, consider routes that ascend into the Sierra de las Labradas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305941795/basilia-s-balcony-and-coto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilia's Balcony and Coto&lt;/a&gt; trail is known to include viewpoints, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Arrabalde?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305549898/arrabalde-circular-through-the-sierra-de-las-labradas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore the elevated terrain and return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What interesting landmarks can I see along the hiking trails around Arrabalde?
A: Many trails offer glimpses of historical and natural landmarks. You might encounter structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eria Bridge in Castrocalbón&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272045033/nogales-monastery-ruins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Monastery Ruins Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6352862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any castles or historical sites to explore near Arrabalde?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt; or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, which often features historical architecture. Other nearby castles include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granucillo Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7400115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanza Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Arrabalde?
A: Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverbanks to the elevated views of the Sierra de las Labradas. The variety of moderate trails and the opportunity to explore historical sites and natural features are frequently highlighted.
tips: A prehistoric rock formation on the outskirts of the town of Arrabalde in the foothills of the Sierra de las Labradas.

A beautiful hiking route along the crest of the Sierra de Carpurias. Panoramic views of the surrounding valleys. The road reaches a small wild rock settlement, called the robbers' fort, at an altitude of 1,000 meters above sea level.
At an altitude of 1000 meters above sea level, there is a rocky outcrop, used to house animals and smugglers. Spectacular views of Arrabalde below.
Arrabalde is a small town located between the Eria River and the Sierra de Carpurias that rises above it at 1000 meters above sea level. Head to the main square to admire the local church.
